## I. Non-AI Pathway

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META:** The skeptics. Not a threat model but the bet that none fire — three distinct reasons behind one low number. LeCun's real worry (concentration) is off-tree. Links: LeCun only.
:::

This is the skeptic's branch — Post 2's "non-believer": the worlds where AI does not raise the danger. A low p(doom) is an output, not a position, and three different bets produce it, sharing a conclusion and almost nothing else. Yann LeCun makes the architectural bet, that large language models are [token-interpolators without the world-models, memory, or planning](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf) the dangerous scenarios require, so the dangerous system never arrives; he has called the extinction worry ["complete B.S."](https://techcrunch.com/2024/10/12/metas-yann-lecun-says-worries-about-a-i-s-existential-threat-are-complete-b-s/) That is not optimism about alignment but a bet that the thing never shows up, and his real worry sits off this tree, in the concentration of AI power he calls the true risk. A second camp grants that the system arrives and bets it stays controllable. A third, the accelerationists, grants that it arrives and bets it is good — the reverse of LeCun on whether it comes, yet the same low number. Post 2 left one more position here: that AI barely moves the needle, and the old risks, nuclear war and pandemics, still dominate.

This branch also marks the edge of the map. Change what "doom" means and you step off the tree: the present-harms camp puts the catastrophe in the present, in concentration and displacement, and the suffering-risk camp in mass suffering rather than extinction. Both are coherent, and both sit on the perimeter rather than on a branch.
